docs.title: Convergio AI
docs.text:
Build intelligent applications with a unified API.

Convergio AI is an API-first platform designed for developers who need powerful, scalable AI capabilities without the infrastructure overhead. Built with FastAPI, it delivers high-performance endpoints with enterprise-grade reliability.

  • Getting Started

    Set up your environment and make your first API call in under five minutes.

    Quickstart

  • API Reference

    Complete reference for all endpoints, authentication, and error handling.

    Explore the API

  • Architecture

    Understand the platform's design, components, and how they connect.

    Platform overview

  • Guides

    Step-by-step guides for deployment, scaling, and best practices.

    View guides

A Digitech Nomads project. Produced by Instinct Bits Lab.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
The Convergio AI API is organized around REST. It accepts JSON-encoded request bodies, returns JSON-encoded responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes.

Base URL:

https://api.convergioai.com/v1

docs.title: Sections
docs.text:
• Authentication

    API key management and request signing.

    Authentication

  • Endpoints

    All available API endpoints with request/response schemas.

    Endpoints

  • Error Handling

    Error codes, formats, and retry strategies.

    Errors

  • Rate Limits

    Throughput limits, headers, and best practices.

    Rate Limits

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All API requests must include a valid API key in the Authorization header.

Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY

docs.title: Obtaining an API key
docs.text:
API keys can be generated from the Convergio AI dashboard.

Security

  • Rotate keys regularly.
  • Never expose keys in client-side code.
  • Use environment variables in production.

This page will be expanded

Detailed authentication flows, scopes, and OAuth support are coming soon.

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All endpoints are versioned under /v1 and require authentication.

docs.title: Health check
docs.text:
GET /v1/health

Returns the current status of the API.

RequestResponse 
curl https://api.convergioai.com/v1/health

{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "1.0.0"
}

This page will be expanded

Full endpoint documentation with request/response schemas, parameters, and examples is coming soon.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The API uses standard HTTP status codes and returns structured JSON error responses.

docs.title: Error response format
docs.text:
{
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_api_key",
    "message": "The API key provided is invalid.",
    "status": 401
  }
}

docs.title: HTTP status codes
docs.text: Code Meaning Description 200 OK Request succeeded. 400 Bad Request Invalid request body or parameters. 401 Unauthorized Missing or invalid API key. 403 Forbidden Insufficient permissions. 404 Not Found Resource does not exist. 429 Too Many Requests Rate limit exceeded. 500 Internal Server Error Unexpected server-side error.

docs.title: Retry strategy
docs.text:
For 429 and 5xx errors, implement exponential backoff:

import time
import requests

def request_with_retry(url, headers, json, max_retries=3):
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=json)
        if response.status_code in (429, 500, 502, 503):
            time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
            continue
        return response
    return response

This page will be expanded

Detailed error codes per endpoint are coming soon.

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text: The API enforces rate limits to ensure fair usage and platform stability.

docs.title: Response headers
docs.text:
Every API response includes rate limit information:

Header Description X-RateLimit-Limit Maximum requests per window. X-RateLimit-Remaining Requests remaining in the window. X-RateLimit-Reset Unix timestamp when the window resets.

docs.title: Handling rate limits
docs.text:
When you receive a 429 Too Many Requests response, wait until X-RateLimit-Reset before retrying. See Error Handling for a retry implementation example.

This page will be expanded

Tier-specific rate limits and upgrade paths are coming soon.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Everything you need to go from zero to your first API call.

  • Installation

    Install the SDK and configure your environment.

    Install

  • Quickstart

    Make your first API call in under five minutes.

    Quickstart

  • Configuration

    Configure authentication, environment variables, and runtime options.

    Configure

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All API requests require a valid API key. Set it as an environment variable:

export CONVERGIOAI_API_KEY="your-api-key-here"

Or pass it directly in your code:

from convergioai import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key-here")

Keep your API key secure

Never commit API keys to version control. Use environment variables or a secrets manager in production.

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text: Variable Description Default CONVERGIOAI_API_KEY Your API authentication key — CONVERGIOAI_BASE_URL API base URL https://api.convergioai.com/v1 CONVERGIOAI_TIMEOUT Request timeout in seconds 30

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
Practical, step-by-step guides for common workflows.

  • Deployment

    Deploy Convergio AI in production environments.

    Deployment guide

  • Best Practices

    Production patterns for reliability, security, and performance.

    Best practices

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text: Recommendations for building robust applications with Convergio AI.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Never hardcode API keys. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
  • Rotate keys regularly. Generate new keys and deprecate old ones on a schedule.
  • Use HTTPS exclusively. All API communication must use TLS.

docs.title: Reliability
docs.text:
• Implement retries with backoff. Handle transient 429 and 5xx errors gracefully. See Error Handling.
  • Set timeouts. Configure reasonable request timeouts to prevent hanging connections.
  • Monitor rate limit headers. Track usage against limits proactively.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Use connection pooling. Reuse HTTP connections to reduce latency.
  • Batch requests when possible. Minimize round trips for bulk operations.
  • Cache responses. Cache deterministic responses to reduce API calls.

This page will be expanded

Detailed patterns with code examples for each recommendation are coming soon.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: This guide covers deploying Convergio AI in production environments.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
docker pull instinctbits/convergioai:latest
docker run -p 8000:8000 -e CONVERGIOAI_API_KEY=your-key instinctbits/convergioai:latest

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Set these in your deployment environment:

Variable Required Description CONVERGIOAI_API_KEY Yes API authentication key DATABASE_URL Yes PostgreSQL connection URI REDIS_URL No Redis connection URI LOG_LEVEL No Logging level (default: info)

This page will be expanded

Kubernetes manifests, Docker Compose configurations, and cloud-specific deployment guides are coming soon.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Convergio AI is built as a modular, API-first platform using FastAPI. This page describes the high-level system design.

docs.title: High-level overview
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Client] -->|HTTPS| B[API Gateway]
    B --> C[Auth Service]
    B --> D[Core API — FastAPI]
    D --> E[Processing Engine]
    D --> F[Data Store]
    E --> G[Model Registry]

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text: Principle Description API-first Every capability is exposed through a versioned REST API. Modular Services are loosely coupled and independently deployable. Observable Structured logging, metrics, and tracing are built in. Secure by default Authentication required on all endpoints. TLS everywhere.

docs.title: Technology stack
docs.text:
Layer Technology API FastAPI Runtime Python 3.12 Data PostgreSQL Cache Redis Deployment Docker

This page will be expanded

docs.title: Python SDK
docs.text:
pip install convergioai

from convergioai import Client

client = Client()
response = client.predict(input="Hello, Convergio!")
print(response)

For detailed SDK documentation, see the Quickstart.
